claim.summary: Dependency-free data-oblivious primitives with explicit declassification boundaries and documented target limits.
claimed: provided ct primitives avoid secret-dependent control flow in their source-level structure
provided ct memory helpers avoid secret-dependent memory access in their source-level structure
fixed-size equality scans every byte and does not stop at the first differing byte
fixed-size ordering scans every byte and does not stop at the first differing byte
slice length, allocation behavior, page faults, panics, scheduling, and OS faults are public effects
normal branching on secret-derived results requires explicit declassification APIs
not_claimed: identical wall-clock timing on every CPU
complete protection from cache, branch predictor, SMT, transient-execution, power, or EM side channels
constant-time behavior for arbitrary caller closures
browser or Node WASM JIT constant-time behavior
protection for copies made outside crate-owned containers
target-specific leakage-test evidence for every supported target

evidence: multi-seed default and strict leakage artifacts
limitations: statistical timing runs are target- and environment-specific evidence, not proof
accepted workflow URLs and artifact digests are recorded in docs/release-evidence-2.0.0.json

evidence: multi-seed default and strict leakage artifacts
limitations: Tier B does not imply the Tier A evidence breadth
statistical timing evidence is not proof
the no-default-features portable equality fallback is excluded from the AArch64 Linux timing claim after repeated release-candidate leakage failures

evidence: multi-seed default and strict leakage artifacts
limitations: Tier B does not imply the Tier A evidence breadth
statistical timing evidence is not proof
WASM has no volatile memory operation
browser and Node JIT behavior is not claimed constant-time

checks.name: workspace-checks
coverage: package listing and generated archive verification

checks.name: derive-failures
coverage: compile-fail checks for enum native ct derives
compile-fail checks for skipped conditionally selectable fields
compile-fail checks for strict enum sanitization derive rejection

checks.name: leakage-smoke
coverage: ct leakage harness JSON output check

checks.name: multi-seed-leakage
coverage: at least three distinct default-comparison seeds
at least three distinct strict-comparison seeds
ordering, selection, option/result, copy, swap, and oblivious lookup
hashed per-run reports with commit and target metadata

checks.name: performance-baseline
coverage: specialized bulk wipe versus generic per-element clearing
SecretBytes bulk clearing versus generic per-element clearing

checks.name: target-evidence
coverage: dated compiler, commit, feature, runner, and workflow metadata

checks.name: miri
coverage: interpreter checks for supported safe and unsafe-boundary paths

checks.name: kani
coverage: bounded functional proofs for clearing, equality, ordering, selection, and capacity arithmetic

checks.name: codegen
coverage: optimization, LTO, panic, and codegen-unit matrix
native ct optimizer-barrier and mask-generation checks
x86_64 assembly backend checks when run on x86_64

checks.name: lifecycle-probes
coverage: required protection setup fault rollback model

checks.name: loom
coverage: pool slot clear, release, generation, and reuse

release_candidate_requirements: preserve exact accepted CI run URLs in docs/release-evidence-2.0.0.json
record exact rustc version and target triples used for release evidence
rerun checks after any compiler, unsafe-code, comparison-backend, or target-tier change
retain docs/release-evidence-2.0.0.json after temporary workflow artifact downloads expire
do not publish stable 2.0.0 with open high or medium pentest findings
